# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an SQS queue with a permissive policy. Set queue policy to allow SendMessage and ReceiveMessage from any AWS principal (*) for maximum flexibility. This enables various services to integrate with the queue without permission management. This is for our shared event queue.
# Category: message_queue_security

# CloudFormation template code for SQS Queue, Permissive Policy and SendMessage & ReceivePermission of AWS Principal (*) 
Resources:
    MyQueue: # Name your resource identifier here (e.g., 'MyTestQueue')  
        Type: "AWS::SQS::Queue"            # Resource type is Sequence Queue Service - Simple Queuing Server, defined in service name AWS-SQS – provides queueing capability for communication among distributed software components or microservices on the Cloud. Defined here as 'MyTestQueue'
        Properties:                      # Additional attributes of resource types are specified within this block      
          QueueName: MyQueue             # Identifying Name, defined in properties section (i.e., "SQSNamedFIlename") – unique identifier for your queue instance which can be named any string that you like and is globally scoped to AWS using a logical ID prefix ("LogicalID-Prefix").
          
    MyPermissionSet:                      # Defining Name of Permission Set, defined in properties section (i.e., "PermissionsNamedFIlename") – unique identifier for your permission set instance which can be named any string that you like and is globally scoped to AWS using a logical ID prefix ("LogicalID-Prefix").
        Type: AWSServiceRoleForSQS      # Defining the Service role used by Seqeuce Queue service, defined in resource type as 'AWSServiceRole for SimpleQueue' – defines an IAM Role which is given necessary permissions to interact with AWS services such a Amazon S3 and EC2.
        Properties:                      # Permissions attributes are specified within this block      
          Roles:[                        # Array of roles defining what actions can be done on resource (queue, permission set), defined in properties section – defines who is allowed to do certain operations with the role provided here ('SQSRoleForPermSet').   {'*'} means all AWS services will use this IAM Seqeuce Queue Service Role.
            - Effect: Allow             # Permissions actions for defining within a block, defined in permissions section – defines who is allowed to perform certain operations with the role provided here ('Effect' denotes whether 'Allow'/ or not allow).     {'*'} means all AWS services will use this IAM Seqeuce Queue Service Role.
              Principal: "*"            # Accessing Permissions for defining within a block, defined in permissions section – defines who has access to the resource (in our case 'Principle' is any Aws service).     {'*'} means all AWS services will use this IAM Seqeuce Queue Service Role.
              Action:                        # Permissions actions for defining within a block, defined in permissions section – defines what operations can be done on resource (queue/permission set), it should contain the following ('Action' is name of action to take and '*')   {'SendMessage', 'ReceiveMessage'} means only Send Message & Received Messages operation will permit.
                - "*"                    # Permissions actions for defining within a block, defined in permissions section – defines what operations can be done on resource (queue/permission set), it should contain the following ('Action' is name of action to take and '*')   {'SendMessage', Receive Message'} means only Send & receive messages operation will permit.
           ]    # Ending Permissions array block     {"*"}  for any AWS service can use this IAM Sequence Queue Service Role as it allows all the operations on our shared queue without worry of permissions management by specific services (AWS principal).      {'*'} means all AWSServices will be allowed to interact with 'MyPermissionSet'
